design_summary.fire_key_persistence: Stored in state['fired_keys_today'], reloaded from disk every daemon tick, reset on date rollover. Replaces the old in-memory-only last_fired/last_fired_date dicts, which reset to empty on every process restart.
design_summary.default_policy: No catch-up (condition='never', window=0 minutes) for any job without an explicit policy entry — requirement 2 (conservative default).
design_summary.double_fire_prevention: SKIPPED_ALREADY_FIRED status returned whenever fire_key is already in fired_keys_today, regardless of how many times evaluate_job_fire is called for the same slot — requirement 6.

policy_highlights.paper_lifecycle_proof_family: Catch-up requires market open AND both reports/latest/broker_truth/broker_truth_summary.json AND reports/latest/instrument_tradability/instrument_tradability_summary.json to exist — never catches up on broker/contract assumption alone.
policy_highlights.auto_retrain: Catch-up requires state/retrain_signal.json to actually exist — never blindly retrains.
policy_highlights.paper_day_proof_pack: Requires dashboard_browser_proof/status.json AND performance_benchmark artifacts to exist first.
policy_highlights.pre_market_jobs: Hard-blocked from catching up once market has opened (09:15 IST) — never run stale pre-market checks mid-session.
policy_highlights.no_policy_default: Any job added to system3_job_scheduler.json without a corresponding entry in the catch-up policy gets zero catch-up window by default — fails safe, not open.

verification: 14 new unit tests in tests/test_scheduler_catchup.py, all passing. See test_summary.json.
